[123:52]We gather here today in reverence joined in our shared hopes and dreams for our beloved nation

[124:00]In this sacred moment of the inauguration of President Donald J. Trump and Vice President J.D. Vance,

[124:07]we turn our hearts to you, seeking your divine assistance and abundant blessings upon this pivotal moment in history.

[124:18]We come before you with profound gratitude for the many gifts you have bestowed upon our land.

[124:24]Thank you for the freedoms we cherish, for the strength of our communities, and for the resilience of our spirit.

[124:32]As our president and vice president embrace their newly appointed roles,

[124:37]we humbly implore that your everlasting love and wisdom will envelop them.

[124:45]Grant them the clarity of mind to navigate the challenges that lie ahead.

[124:50]and the compassion to serve all citizens with fairness and integrity.

[124:56]May their hearts be filled with a giving spirit

[124:59]and sincere understanding for those whom they represent.

[125:04]May they be beacons of hope in times of uncertainty

[125:08]and prophetic voices in defending the dignity of all created life.

[125:16]We pray for a spirit of collaboration to flourish in our government and across our nation,

[125:22]fostering an environment where dialogue and heartfelt listening will prevail over division or discord.

[125:43]we also seek your comfort, O God,

[131:24]And I believe that for the past six to nine months, the mainstream media has been conditioning him to act differently.

[131:33]He's not tweeting anymore.

[131:36]His speeches, at least in the final months of the campaign, were not as incisive or divisive as they used to be.

[132:01]that he went from American carnage to unity and our future together.

[132:14]If you remember in Trump's first inaugural in 2017, he had a very famous line.

[132:21]He said, American carnage. He said, abandoned factories are like tombstones across the landscape.

[132:29]And that phrase, American carnage in particular, the media seized on that and said that was emblematic of a very dark tone.

[132:40]And so, like I said, I think in the past year, the media has been conditioning Trump to be more unifying and sunny and optimistic.

[132:52]And so they said his inaugural specifically would be a departure from that phrase from the first one.

[142:00]That is what Trump's cabinet wants to do, just like the first time.

[142:04]Trump is a protectionist.

[142:07]He likes McKinley because McKinley was a protectionist.

[142:10]Trump wants to make tariffs part of our economy.

[142:14]And it's good for multiple reasons.

[142:44]And they will quickly be removed

[142:47]And that's a conflict of vision.

[142:52]I think he's always been strongest on trade.

[142:57]is because tariffs can be applied unilaterally.
